openFindings.title: F005 remains open: spec.md still mixes every-startup semantics with one-time and first-launch wording
openFindings.description: The runtime, checklist, and changelog now match the every-startup idempotent retry contract, but spec.md still describes the drop as one-time / first launch in multiple places.

transitions.reason: Prior closure blocker not actually fixed because spec.md still contradicts the shipped contract

openFindings.title: F006 remains open: memory/save.md still uses retired continuity-artifact framing
openFindings.description: The memory save command still tells operators about continuity artifacts and standalone continuity artifacts instead of only canonical packet docs and _memory.continuity.

transitions.reason: Prior closure blocker not actually fixed because save.md still preserves retired continuity-artifact wording

openFindings.title: NF002 remains open: README.txt and confirm workflow wording still diverge from the runtime contract
openFindings.description: manage.md and most workflow YAMLs are aligned, but README.txt still uses continuity-artifact framing and the plan/implement confirm variants still use generated continuity artifact wording, so the prior expected-closed drift remains blocker-class.

transitions.reason: Broader command-doc workflow contract still diverges from runtime discovery wording
Workflow drift is narrower than v4 but still present in plan_confirm and implement_confirm wording
Packet severity rule treats prior expected-closed findings still open as blockers

openFindings.title: F003 reopened: cross-runtime deep-review manuals diverge on the iteration artifact schema
openFindings.description: .claude/.gemini use the canonical reducer-readable iteration template, while .opencode/.codex still prescribe the older Scope/Scorecard/Cross-Reference format.

transitions.reason: Cross-runtime manual parity drift reappeared after the remediation sweep
Packet severity rule treats prior expected-closed findings still open as blockers

openFindings.title: F012 remains open: master feature catalog still describes older incremental scan mechanics
openFindings.description: The consolidated catalog describes older mtime-only/cooldown scan mechanics while the dedicated entry and handler now describe content-hash-sensitive bucketing plus an atomic scan lease.

transitions.reason: Master feature catalog did not fully catch up to the current lease-based discovery mechanics

openFindings.title: F013 remains open: operator-facing verification surfaces do not fully reflect the three-source scan contract
openFindings.description: The reviewed catalog entries describe three scan sources, including graph metadata, but the handler hints and summary playbook coverage do not surface that full contract consistently.

transitions.reason: Feature catalog and playbook summaries are narrower than the full three-source discovery contract

openFindings.title: F014 remains open: save and handover docs still mix hard-coded /tmp examples with normalized temp-path guidance
openFindings.description: The save workflow already documents the normalized ${TMPDIR:-/tmp} form, but several examples still hard-code /tmp/save-context-data.json.

transitions.reason: Mixed temp-path examples survived the broader continuity cleanup

resolvedFindings.title: F007 closed: the complete workflow pair now routes save and index steps through canonical spec documents
resolvedFindings.notes: The original complete-workflow defect is gone; remaining workflow drift is limited to NF002 prose in the plan/implement confirm variants.

resolvedFindings.title: F010 closed: create folder-readme workflows now route save steps through implementation-summary.md
resolvedFindings.notes: Both folder-readme workflow variants now save/index the canonical implementation-summary.md target and explicitly retire standalone memory/*.md files.

resolvedFindings.title: F009 closed: stale save-pipeline test comment now matches canonical spec-document classification behavior
resolvedFindings.notes: The inline comment now reflects the live canonical spec-document classification logic rather than the retired specs/*/memory pattern.

resolvedFindings.title: F011 closed: create/agent.md prior-session prompt is now canonical-doc based
resolvedFindings.notes: The current wording routes prior-session loading through handover.md, _memory.continuity, and canonical spec docs instead of legacy memory files.
